title: Liveness, Readiness, and Startup Probes — When should you use a liveness probe?
text:
If the process in your container is able to crash on its own whenever it
encounters an issue or becomes unhealthy, you do not necessarily need a liveness
probe; the kubelet will automatically perform the correct action in accordance
with the Pod's
restartPolicy
.
If you'd like your container to be killed and restarted if a probe fails, then
specify a liveness probe, and specify a
restartPolicy
of
Always
or
OnFailure
.
A common pattern for liveness probes is to use the same low-cost HTTP endpoint
as for readiness probes, but with a higher
failureThreshold
. This ensures
that the pod is observed as not-ready for some period of time before it is hard
killed.
